name: Modified Ashworth Scale
description: A clinical scale for grading spasticity and muscle tone. Measures resistance during passive soft-tissue stretching. Used to assess muscle hypertonia in patients with neurological conditions such as stroke, spinal cord injury, multiple sclerosis, and cerebral palsy.
instructions: Place the patient in a supine position. For muscles that primarily flex a joint, place the joint in maximal flexion and move to maximal extension over one second. For muscles that primarily extend a joint, place the joint in maximal extension and move to maximal flexion over one second. Score based on the resistance felt during passive movement.

questions.question: Rate the muscle tone/spasticity during passive movement:

options.label: 1 - Slight increase in muscle tone, manifested by a catch and release or by minimal resistance at the end of ROM
options.description: Slight increase in muscle tone, manifested by a catch and release or by minimal resistance at the end of the range of motion when the affected part(s) is moved in flexion or extension

options.label: 1+ - Slight increase in muscle tone, manifested by a catch, followed by minimal resistance throughout less than half of ROM
options.description: Slight increase in muscle tone, manifested by a catch, followed by minimal resistance throughout the remainder (less than half) of the ROM

options.label: 2 - More marked increase in muscle tone through most of ROM, but affected part(s) easily moved
options.description: More marked increase in muscle tone through most of the ROM, but affected part(s) easily moved

options.label: 3 - Considerable increase in muscle tone, passive movement difficult
options.description: Considerable increase in muscle tone, passive movement difficult

options.label: 4 - Affected part(s) rigid in flexion or extension
options.description: Affected part(s) rigid in flexion or extension

severityBands.label: Mild Spasticity
severityBands.description: Slight increase in muscle tone with catch and/or minimal resistance.

severityBands.label: Moderate Spasticity
severityBands.description: More marked increase in muscle tone but limb easily moved.

severityBands.label: Severe Spasticity
severityBands.description: Considerable increase in tone; passive movement difficult or limb rigid.

interpretation.scoringMethod: Single selection from 0 to 4 (with 1+ between 1 and 2). Score each muscle group separately.

grades.1: Slight increase - catch and release or minimal resistance at end ROM
grades.1+: Slight increase - catch followed by minimal resistance through <50% ROM
grades.2: More marked increase through most of ROM, but easily moved

interpretation.clinicalUse: Used to monitor spasticity changes over time, evaluate treatment effectiveness (botulinum toxin, baclofen, physical therapy), and guide rehabilitation planning.
interpretation.limitations: Ordinal scale with unequal intervals. Inter-rater reliability can vary. Does not distinguish between neural and non-neural components of resistance.

muscleGroups.note: The MAS can be applied to multiple muscle groups. Common assessments include:

references.citation: Bohannon RW, Smith MB. Interrater reliability of a modified Ashworth scale of muscle spasticity. Phys Ther. 1987;67(2):206-207.
Ashworth B. Preliminary trial of carisoprodol in multiple sclerosis. Practitioner. 1964;192:540-542.

metadata.author: Bohannon RW, Smith MB (modified from Ashworth B)
